key_insight: Most enzyme specificity models look strong on standard tests but fail on discovery-relevant settings where the true enzyme or substrate is unseen.
engineering_relevance: For enzyme annotation and biocatalyst discovery, models must rank the correct catalyst among many close candidates, not just classify familiar pairs.
method: Benchmarked four recent enzyme-substrate and enzyme-reaction models on unseen-enzyme/unseen-substrate settings
Built a large cytochrome P450 dataset with 2,922 reactions from 768 enzymes and a native-organism ranking task
Adapted Boltz interaction embeddings with supervised classifiers for enzyme-substrate prediction
evidence_signal: On the new CYP ranking benchmark, most models did not beat BLAST baselines even after fine-tuning, while Boltz-derived features consistently did.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on benchmark design, so results from random-split or familiar-pair evaluations may overestimate real-world usefulness.
paper_title: Rethinking Benchmarks and Models for Enzyme Specificity Prediction
